title: ranunculus cuneilobus var. distrias
intro:
Ranunculus cuneilobus var. distrias, also known as the blue water crowfoot, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and easing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus cuneilobus var. distrias is often used as a decoction or infusion, and it can be made into a salve or ointment to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.

Note: I've provided the available information, but please consult with a medical professional or a qualified botanist before using any plant for medicinal purposes.
